metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the explicit prerequisite dependency: this plan assumes child story 1 of epic jira-reb-686 ('status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion', which registers `idea` in `_VALID_LIST_STATUSES`, `_VALID_STATUSES`, and the reducer) is merged before implementation begins — add this to a 'Prerequisites' or 'Dependencies' section.
findings.finding: The plan's central premise — that `idea` is an existing ticket status that can be guarded with `status == 'idea'` — is unverifiable from the current codebase. The string `'idea'` appears nowhere in the ticket lifecycle code: it is absent from `_VALID_LIST_STATUSES` (reads_cli.py line 71-73), absent from `_VALID_STATUSES` in transition.py (line 29), and has zero occurrences anywhere in src/rebar. The `idea` status is introduced by sibling child story 1 ('status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion') of epic jira-reb-686, which is not yet closed. This plan is written as if that prerequisite is already landed.
findings.location: Why / What sections (throughout) — premise that `idea` is a recognized ticket status
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Prerequisites' section: 'This story depends on child story 1 ("status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion") of epic jira-reb-686 being closed and merged. Until `idea` is registered in `_VALID_LIST_STATUSES` (reads_cli.py), `_VALID_STATUSES` (transition.py), and the ticket schema, no real ticket can carry `status == "idea"` and the per-check guards are untestable end-to-end.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `'idea'` is an already-shipped, schema-registered ticket status (present in `common.schema.json` ticket_status enum and handled by the reducer), or document the prerequisite change that introduces it before these guards can do any work.
findings.finding: The entire exemption mechanism depends on 'idea' being a recognized, persisted ticket status value, but no such status exists in the codebase: `common.schema.json` `ticket_status` enum lists only [open, in_progress, blocked, closed, archived, deleted]; `_commands/transition.py` `_VALID_STATUSES` contains only (open, in_progress, closed, blocked); no reducer, schema, or command file references the string 'idea' as a status; and there is no `rebar idea` command registered in `_cli/__init__.py` or `_commands/`. If 'idea' is a not-yet-implemented status, every `status == "idea"` guard will be unreachable dead code, all ACs will pass vacuously, and the real exemption will never fire on actual idea-type tickets.
findings.location: ## What — 'add a `status == "idea"` skip inside each exempted check'; also Scope: `validate_checks.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Before adding `status == "idea"` guards, verify the status is present in `common.schema.json#/$defs/ticket_status` enum and in `_commands/transition.py _VALID_STATUSES`. If it is instead represented by a tag (e.g. `tag:idea`), the guards must check `"idea" in issue.get("tags", [])` rather than `issue.get("status") == "idea"`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `check_ticket_count` exemption mechanism: since `total = len(issues)` has no per-item loop, specify whether idea tickets are filtered from the `issues` list before the `len()` call (e.g., `total = sum(1 for i in issues if i.get('status') != 'idea')`) rather than using the same per-item `continue` pattern as other checks.
findings.finding: The plan states 'add a `status == "idea"` skip inside each exempted check' as the uniform mechanism, but `check_ticket_count` (validate_checks.py lines 140-161) has no per-item loop — it uses `total = len(issues)` directly. A per-check `status == 'idea'` guard cannot be placed 'inside the check' in the same way as other checks without either (a) adding a pre-count filtering pass over `issues`, or (b) filtering at the call site before passing the list in. The plan's own AC says 'excludes idea tickets from the check_ticket_count total', which implies option (a) — a structurally different operation from the 'skip' described for the other five checks.
findings.location: What section — Exempted checks bullet for `check_ticket_count`; AC item 'validate excludes idea tickets from the check_ticket_count total'; validate_checks.py lines 140-161
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `total = len(issues)` with `total = sum(1 for i in issues if i.get('status') != 'idea')` inside `check_ticket_count`, and update the plan's mechanism description to note this is a count-filter rather than a loop-skip.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the check_ticket_count exemption mechanism: specify that idea tickets should be filtered out of the issues list before the `len()` call (e.g., `total = sum(1 for i in issues if i.get('status') != 'idea')`), distinct from the early-return 'skip' pattern used for the other exempted checks.
findings.finding: The plan's stated implementation mechanism — 'add a status == "idea" skip inside each exempted check' — is ambiguous for check_ticket_count, where the goal is to exclude idea tickets from the total count, not to skip the check entirely. check_ticket_count uses `total = len(issues)` (validate_checks.py line 143); a blanket early-return guard would either skip the whole check (leaving zero output) or leave idea tickets in the count. The AC item says 'excludes idea tickets from the check_ticket_count total,' implying intra-check filtering before the len() call — a structurally different operation from the 'skip' described for other checks.
findings.location: What section — 'Exempted checks' bullet for check_ticket_count; AC item 'validate excludes idea tickets from the check_ticket_count total'; validate_checks.py line 140–161
findings.suggested_fix: In check_ticket_count, replace `total = len(issues)` with `total = sum(1 for i in issues if i.get('status') != 'idea')` and document in the plan that this check uses count-filtering rather than a skip guard.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the `check_ticket_count` exemption is a pre-`len()` filter (e.g., `total = sum(1 for i in issues if i.get('status') != 'idea')`), not a top-level early-return skip, since the function has no per-item loop to attach a `continue` guard to.
findings.finding: The plan's implementation instruction 'add a `status == "idea"` skip inside each exempted check' is structurally ambiguous for `check_ticket_count`, which uses `total = len(issues)` with no per-item loop. An early-return guard would skip the entire check (emitting no findings at all), while the AC states 'validate *excludes idea tickets from the check_ticket_count total*' — implying per-item filtering before the `len()` call. These are two structurally different operations; the plan description fits neither correctly.
findings.location: ## What — 'Exempted checks' bullet for `check_ticket_count`; AC line 'validate excludes idea tickets from the check_ticket_count total'; `validate_checks.py` lines 140–161
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a parenthetical for check_ticket_count: '(for `check_ticket_count`, filter idea tickets before the `len()` call: `total = sum(1 for i in issues if i.get("status") != "idea")`, since there is no per-item loop to short-circuit)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a single exemption predicate or constant (e.g., `UNDESIGNED_STATUSES = frozenset({'idea'})` or a `@skip_for_statuses('idea')` decorator) in `validate_checks.py` and reference it from each exempted check, so the full exemption surface is auditable from one place.
findings.finding: The exemption rule (`status == 'idea'` skips in 5+ individual check functions) is a business rule expected to evolve — new checks may be added, additional draft-like statuses may emerge — but the plan deliberately scatters the guard across multiple function bodies with no centralized registry, decorator, or `EXEMPT_STATUSES` set. This makes auditing the full exemption surface and extending it consistently error-prone.
findings.suggested_fix: Add at the top of `validate_checks.py`: `_UNDESIGNED_STATUSES = frozenset({'idea'})`. Replace each inline `status == 'idea'` guard with `issue.status in _UNDESIGNED_STATUSES`. This costs one line and makes future status additions (e.g., `draft`) a single-point change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/interfaces/queries/test_validate_compute.py` (or a companion file) to the Scope section as a required edit target, covering both the exemption negative cases (ACs 1–6) and the retained-check positive cases (ACs 7–9).
findings.finding: The Scope section names only `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py` as the edit target, but ACs 7, 8, and 9 explicitly require 'test-verified' coverage for the retained checks, and `docs/mutation-testing.md` pins validate health-check changes to `tests/interfaces/queries/test_validate_compute.py` as the mutation gate. The test file is a required edit target that is not enumerated in Scope.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `tests/interfaces/queries/test_validate_compute.py` — add parametrized test cases with `idea`-status synthetic tickets injected via `monkeypatch.setattr(_validate, '_raw_tickets', ...)` matching the `_ticket()` helper pattern already in that file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'test-verified' (or an equivalent proving command such as `pytest tests/... -k idea`) to each of the five exempted-check ACs so that in-session verifiability is explicit and consistent with the retained-check ACs.
findings.finding: Five of the ten ACs (the exempted-check negative cases) lack a stated proving command or 'test-verified' qualifier, while the three retained-check ACs explicitly say 'test-verified'. An executing agent cannot determine whether unit tests are required for the negative ACs or how to produce evidence of compliance.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first five bullets (check_empty_epics, check_missing_descriptions, check_interface_contracts, check_orphaned_tasks including cluster, check_ticket_count)
findings.suggested_fix: Append '(test-verified)' to each of the five negative-case ACs and note that a fixture with an idea-status ticket should be added to the validate_checks test suite alongside the existing retained-check tests.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the What section whether `check_ticket_count` needs a pre-count filter (exclude idea tickets before summing) or a per-item guard, and align the AC wording accordingly.
findings.finding: The `check_ticket_count` AC says 'validate excludes idea tickets from the check_ticket_count total', but the plan's mechanism (per-check `status == "idea"` skip) could mean either: (a) the check is skipped entirely when the current ticket under iteration is an idea, or (b) idea tickets are filtered from the aggregate count before the threshold is evaluated. These produce different behavior if the check operates on a global count rather than per-ticket.
findings.location: What section — 'check_ticket_count (parking-lot tickets are not active load)'; AC bullet 6
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'For check_ticket_count, filter out idea-status tickets from the list before computing the count total, rather than using a per-item skip.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what `type` field idea tickets carry (e.g., always `epic`, or can be any type) so it is clear whether the `check_missing_descriptions` exemption is exercised by the type guard alone or genuinely requires the new `status == 'idea'` guard.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `check_missing_descriptions` needs a `status == 'idea'` skip, but the check already contains an explicit type guard (`if issue.get('type', ...) != 'task': continue`, validate_checks.py line 265) that skips any issue that is not of type `task`. The plan never states what `type` field idea tickets carry. If `idea` tickets are always created with `type == 'epic'` (as the plan's 'idea command' description implies — 'an empty epic'), `check_missing_descriptions` already never fires on them. Whether the exemption is necessary depends entirely on whether idea tickets can have `type == 'task'`, which the plan does not specify.
findings.location: What section — Exempted checks bullet for `check_missing_descriptions`; AC item 'validate emits no check_missing_descriptions finding for an idea ticket with an empty description'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `'idea'` has already been added to `common.schema.json`'s `ticket_status` enum and `types.py`'s `TicketStatus` Literal by a prerequisite plan; if not, add `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` and `src/rebar/types.py` to the Scope as required edit targets.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `'idea'` as a new `status` discriminator used in 5+ `if status == 'idea'` guards in `validate_checks.py`, but `'idea'` is absent from the canonical `ticket_status` enum in `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (verified: enum is `["open", "in_progress", "blocked", "closed", "archived", "deleted"]`) and from the `TicketStatus` Literal in `src/rebar/types.py`. The plan names only `validate_checks.py` as the edit target and never mentions the schema or types as needing an update.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"idea"` to the `ticket_status` enum array in `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` and regenerate `src/rebar/types.py` via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`. If a prerequisite plan already covers this, cite it explicitly in the plan's Scope section.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
